rules.title: 4D Boundary Check
rules.applicability: L4 sub-process step must operate within a single 4D boundary (department, system, time, location).

rules.title: 4D Attribution Check
rules.applicability: Every L4 step must have explicit attribution for all four dimensions (who, which system, when, where).

rules.title: Terminal Org Check
rules.applicability: L4 steps must not route to organizational units that have no further defined actions.

rules.title: Cross-Org Exceptions
rules.applicability: Cross-organizational handoffs must be explicitly documented with exception handling.

rules.title: Quantity Consistency
rules.applicability: Input and output quantities referenced in L4 steps must be consistent across connected steps.

rules.title: Wait Step Validation
rules.applicability: Wait or hold steps must have explicit timeout or escalation conditions defined.

rules.title: Duplicate Entry Detection
rules.applicability: L4 flow must not contain duplicate entry points for the same trigger condition.

rules.title: System Switch Check
rules.applicability: Transitions between systems within a single L4 step must be explicitly documented.

rules.title: Unnecessary Approval Detection
rules.applicability: Approval steps that add no business value or duplicate prior approvals should be flagged.

rules.title: L4 Step Completeness
rules.applicability: Every L4 step must have a name, assigned role, input, output, and successor or terminal condition.

rules.title: Single Main Role
rules.applicability: Each L5 sub-process must have exactly one main Responsible role.

rules.title: Business Output Four Questions
rules.applicability: Each L5 output must answer: What is delivered? To whom? In what format? By when?

rules.title: Verb-Object Naming Convention
rules.applicability: L5 process names must follow verb-object pattern (e.g. 'Approve Request', 'Validate Data').

rules.title: R0 Anti-Pattern: Missing Input
rules.applicability: L5 process must not start without a clearly defined trigger or input artifact.

rules.title: R1 Anti-Pattern: Orphan Output
rules.applicability: L5 output must connect to a downstream consumer or be a final deliverable.

rules.title: R2 Anti-Pattern: Role Mismatch
rules.applicability: The assigned role must be capable of performing the described action in the given system context.

rules.title: R3 Anti-Pattern: System Mismatch
rules.applicability: The referenced system must support the described operation for the assigned role.

rules.title: IPO Structure Check
rules.applicability: Each L5 process must have identifiable Input, Process, and Output structure.

rules.title: Good Product Conditions
rules.applicability: L5 outputs must define what constitutes a good product: measurable quality criteria.

rules.title: Tool Decoupling
rules.applicability: L5 process description should describe business logic independent of specific tool implementations.

rules.title: One-Breath Granularity
rules.applicability: Each L6 step must be describable in one breath (approximately 15 seconds of speaking).

rules.title: Verb-Object Naming at L6
rules.applicability: L6 step names must follow verb-object pattern consistently with parent L5 naming.

rules.title: Business Semantics Only
rules.applicability: L6 steps must describe business actions, not technical system operations.

rules.title: Tool Leakage Detection
rules.applicability: L6 steps must not contain references to specific tool names, API endpoints, or database tables.

rules.title: Role Leakage Detection
rules.applicability: L6 steps must not embed role assignments that belong to the L4 or L5 layer.

rules.title: L6 Step Completeness
rules.applicability: Each L6 step must have a name, input reference, output reference, and link to parent L5.

rules.title: Scenario Context Required
rules.applicability: Every SOP must declare the business scenario or context in which it applies.

rules.title: Five Signals Check
rules.applicability: SOP must identify trigger signals: who, what, when, where, and why for each procedure entry.

rules.title: Specialization Fields
rules.applicability: SOP must specify specialization fields such as applicable product lines, regions, or departments.

rules.title: Non-Empty L6 Reference
rules.applicability: Every SOP entry must reference at least one L6 step or explain why no L6 decomposition exists.

rules.title: SOP Attribution
rules.applicability: SOP entries must attribute ownership to a specific role or organizational unit.

rules.title: Reference Validity
rules.applicability: All references from SOP to L4/L5/L6 nodes must resolve to existing nodes in the architecture model.

rules.title: Applicability Scope
rules.applicability: SOP must declare its applicability scope: universal, conditional, or exception-only.

rules.title: Orphan Node Detection
rules.applicability: Every node (except root) must have a valid parent reference. Orphan nodes are not allowed.

rules.title: Dangling Reference Detection
rules.applicability: All parent_id and relationship references must point to existing nodes.

rules.title: Cycle Detection
rules.applicability: The hierarchy must be a directed acyclic graph. No cycles are permitted.

rules.title: Fan-Out Limit
rules.applicability: A single node should not have an excessive number of children (warn above 10, block above 20).

rules.title: Attribution Conflict
rules.applicability: Child node attribution must not contradict parent node attribution in the same dimension.

rules.title: Coverage Completeness
rules.applicability: All leaf nodes at the lowest declared level must be reachable from the root.

rules.title: Output Chain Continuity
rules.applicability: Outputs from child nodes must chain into the parent node's output or a sibling node's input.

rules.title: Layer Skip Detection
rules.applicability: A node must not skip a layer in the hierarchy (e.g. L3 directly containing L6).

rules.title: Naming Consistency
rules.applicability: Child node names should be consistent in style with their parent node names.

rules.title: Version Consistency
rules.applicability: All nodes in a single architecture model must belong to the same version.

rules.title: Start Event Presence
rules.applicability: Every BPMN process must have at least one start event.

rules.title: End Event Presence
rules.applicability: Every BPMN process must have at least one end event.

rules.title: Event Type Declaration
rules.applicability: All events in a BPMN diagram must have a declared event definition type.

rules.title: Gateway Pairing
rules.applicability: Split gateways must have a corresponding merge gateway of the same type.

rules.title: Default Flow on Exclusive Gateway
rules.applicability: Every exclusive gateway with multiple outgoing flows must designate exactly one default flow.

rules.title: Dangling Sequence Flow
rules.applicability: Every sequence flow must have valid source and target references.

rules.title: Orphan Task Detection
rules.applicability: Every task, sub-process, and event must be connected to at least one sequence flow.

rules.title: Pool and Lane Usage
rules.applicability: In multi-pool diagrams, all tasks must be assigned to a lane within a pool.

rules.title: Sequence Flow vs Message Flow Distinction
rules.applicability: Sequence flows must connect elements within the same pool; message flows must connect across pools.

rules.title: Sub-Process Boundary Completeness
rules.applicability: Sub-processes should have clearly defined input and output connections.

rules.title: Exception and Error Path
rules.applicability: Processes with external interactions should define exception or error handling paths.

rules.title: Rollback Path Presence
rules.applicability: Multi-step transactional processes should define rollback or compensation paths.

rules.title: Task Label Completeness
rules.applicability: All tasks in a BPMN diagram must have non-empty names.

rules.title: Intermediate Event Placement
rules.applicability: Intermediate events should be placed at meaningful positions in the process flow.

rules.title: Data Object Association
rules.applicability: Data objects should be associated with at least one task or sub-process.

rules.title: Line Crossing Detection
rules.applicability: Flow lines should not cross each other.

rules.title: Flow Direction Consistency
rules.applicability: All sequence flows should follow a consistent primary direction.

rules.title: Backflow Detection
rules.applicability: Flows should not go backward relative to the primary reading direction.

rules.title: Diagram Density
rules.applicability: Diagrams should not contain excessive elements in a single view.

rules.title: Label Readability
rules.applicability: All element labels must be readable with sufficient font size and no truncation.

rules.title: Spacing Consistency
rules.applicability: Elements should have consistent spacing throughout the diagram.

rules.title: Color Dependency
rules.applicability: Diagrams must not rely solely on color to convey meaning.

rules.title: Legend Presence
rules.applicability: Diagrams with custom symbols or colors should include a legend.

rules.title: Title and Metadata
rules.applicability: Diagrams should have a title or caption identifying the process.

rules.title: Minimum Element Separation
rules.applicability: Adjacent elements must have sufficient separation to be visually distinguishable.

rules.title: L4 to Sub-Process Mapping
rules.applicability: Every L4 sub-process node must have a corresponding SUB_PROCESS in the diagram.

rules.title: L5 to Task Mapping
rules.applicability: Every L5 process node must have a corresponding TASK in the diagram.

rules.title: Role to Lane Mapping
rules.applicability: Roles assigned to architecture nodes should correspond to lanes in the diagram.

rules.title: Deliverable to Data Object Mapping
rules.applicability: Input and output deliverables should have corresponding DATA_OBJECT elements in the diagram.

rules.title: Cross-Org Message Flow Mapping
rules.applicability: Cross-organizational interactions should be represented as MESSAGE_FLOW elements.

rules.title: Exception Path Mapping
rules.applicability: Exception handling paths in the architecture should be represented in the diagram.

rules.title: Architecture Completeness Coverage
rules.applicability: All L4 and L5 architecture nodes must be represented in the diagram model.

rules.title: Diagram Extra Elements
rules.applicability: Diagram elements without matching architecture nodes may indicate undocumented processes.
